themes: Beyond Context Limits: Subconscious Threads for Long-Horizon Reasoning
Human-Inspired Episodic Memory for Infinite Context LLMs (EM-LLM)
Peripheral (of interest but outside core themes)

items.title: Neuroscience-Inspired Artificial Intelligence
items.memo: Hassabis, Kumaran, Summerfield, Botvinick. Landmark NeuroAI manifesto
items.tldr: It is argued that better understanding biological brains could play a vital role in building intelligent machines in humans and other animals.

items.title: Catalyzing next-generation Artificial Intelligence through NeuroAI
items.memo: Zador et al. NeuroAI roadmap — next AI breakthrough through neuroscience
items.tldr: An expanded version of the Turing test that involves embodied sensorimotor interactions with the world as a new framework for accelerating progress in artificia

items.title: Leveraging insights from neuroscience to build adaptive artificial intelligence
items.memo: Mathis 2026. Latest position on neuroscience principles → adaptive AI design
items.tldr: The behavioral and neural foundations of adaptive biological intelligence are reviewed, parallel progress in AI is examined, and brain-inspired approaches for b

items.title: A Framework for Intelligence and Cortical Function Based on Grid Cells in the Neocortex
items.memo: Hawkins, Lewis, Klukas, Purdy & Ahmad (Numenta). Original grid cell-based intelligence framework

items.title: Are Grid-Like Representations a Component of All Perception and Cognition?
items.memo: Chen (Zhe Sage). Argues grid-like representations play a universal role in perception and cognition
items.tldr: It is argued and reason the rationale why grid responses may be universal in the brain for a wide range of perceptual and cognitive tasks that involve locomotio

items.title: The Thousand Brains Project: A New Paradigm for Sensorimotor Intelligence
items.memo: Clay, Leadholm & Hawkins (Numenta). Cortical column-based sensorimotor intelligence

items.subtheme: 2a. Grid Cells, Spatial Coding & Reference Frames

items.title: How to Represent Part-Whole Hierarchies in a Neural Network (GLOM)
items.memo: Hinton. Part-whole hierarchical representation theory proposal (not an implementation — as explicitly stated in the paper) — cortical column perspective
items.tldr: If GLOM can be made to work, it should significantly improve the interpretability of the representations produced by transformer-like systems when applied to vi

items.title: The Platonic Representation Hypothesis
items.memo: Huh, Cheung, Wang, Isola (MIT/CSAIL). Neural representations across heterogeneous modalities converge at scale — the **Platonic Representation Hypothesis** (official paper title). ⭐ Selected
items.tldr: It is argued that representations in AI models, particularly deep networks, are converging, and hypothesize that this convergence is driving toward a shared sta

items.title: DreamSim: Learning New Dimensions of Human Visual Similarity using Synthetic Data
items.memo: Fu, Tamir, Sundaram, Shechtman, Shechtman, Hertzmann, Zhang, Isola. New perceptual similarity metric trained on synthetic data — captures mid-level human visual similarity (foreground/structure/pose). Cross-modal representation convergence context. DOI: 10.5555/3666122.3668330. ⭐ Selected
items.tldr: A perceptual metric is developed that assesses images holistically and focuses heavily on foreground objects and semantic content while also being sensitive to

items.title: Transformers are SSMs: Generalized Models via Structured State Space Duality (Mamba-2)
items.memo: Dao & Gu. Unified theory of SSMs and Transformers — based on Mamba-2. ⭐ Selected
items.tldr: The state space duality (SSD) framework allows us to design a new architecture (Mamba-2) whose core layer is an a refinement of Mamba's selective SSM that is 2-

items.title: Gated Delta Networks: Improving Mamba2 with Delta Rule
items.memo: Yang et al. Introduces Delta Rule into Mamba-2 — connects to biological Hebbian learning. ⭐ Selected
items.tldr: This work introduces the gated delta rule and develops a parallel training algorithm optimized for modern hardware, which consistently surpasses existing models

items.title: Perceiver IO: Structured Inputs & Outputs
items.memo: Universal I/O architecture for arbitrary modalities — representational universality perspective
items.tldr: This work proposes Perceiver IO, a general-purpose architecture that handles data from arbitrary settings while scaling linearly with the size of inputs and out

items.title: Why there are complementary learning systems in the hippocampus and neocortex (CLS original)
items.memo: McClelland, McNaughton, O'Reilly. Original CLS theory paper — theoretical foundation for H-SSM
items.tldr: The account presented here suggests that memories are first stored via synaptic changes in the hippocampal system, that these changes support reinstatement of r
items.subtheme: 3a. [Neuro] Biological Memory: CLS, Engrams & Hippocampal Computation

items.title: Complementary learning systems within the hippocampus (Updated CLS)
items.memo: Kumaran, Hassabis, McClelland. Modernized CLS — schema-dependent fast learning, bidirectional interactions
items.tldr: This work updates complementary learning systems (CLS) theory and extends it by showing that recurrent activation of hippocampal traces can support some forms o
items.subtheme: 3a. [Neuro] Biological Memory: CLS, Engrams & Hippocampal Computation

items.title: Rapid consolidation to hippocampus and cortex (Schema Consolidation)
items.memo: Tse et al. Prior schema enables rapid neocortical learning — key evidence for updated CLS
items.tldr: It is reported that systems consolidation can occur extremely quickly if an associative “schema” into which new information is incorporated has previously been
items.subtheme: 3a. [Neuro] Biological Memory: CLS, Engrams & Hippocampal Computation

items.title: Human-Inspired Episodic Memory for Infinite Context LLMs (EM-LLM)
items.memo: Episodic memory-based infinite context — directly tied to NeuroAI. Detailed review in §below

items.title: Titans: Learning to Memorize at Test Time
items.memo: Behrouz et al. (Google). Fast/slow dual memory structure — connects to CLS. Detailed review in §below

items.title: From Tokens to Thoughts: How LLMs and Humans Trade Compression for Meaning
items.memo: Shani (Stanford). Token-to-thought compression-meaning tradeoff (Information Bottleneck framework). Connected to cognitive science. (ICLR 2026 acceptance unconfirmed — classified as preprint)
items.tldr: An Information Bottleneck framework is applied to compare human conceptual structure with embeddings from 40+ LLMs using classic categorization benchmarks, find

items.title: Training LLMs to Reason in Continuous Latent Space (Coconut)
items.memo: Hao et al. Reasoning in continuous latent space — starting point for Reasoning by Superposition. Detailed review in §below

items.title: Emotion concepts and their function in a large language model
items.memo: Sofroniew (Anthropic). Functional analysis of emotion concepts inside LLMs — internal representations causally influence Claude's behavior. arXiv 2604.07729, blog 2026-04-02

items.title: Emergence of Language in the Developing Brain
items.memo: Evanson et al. 7400+ electrode iEEG, 46 participants. Hierarchical language development from ages 2-5 to adulthood. (French clinical neuroscience consortium)

items.title: Neuroscience Needs Behavior: Correcting a Reductionist Bias
items.memo: Krakauer, Ghazanfar, Gomez-Marin, MacIver, Poeppel. Argues neural circuit analysis alone is insufficient — behavior must be the primary explanandum. Foundational manifesto for computational neuroethology.
